title: Blepharitis
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports specific visual, pain, photophobia, trauma, corneal, and pupil warning prompts only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

assessment.text: Supports blepharitis identity, watery or red eyes, gritty, burning, stinging, greasy, itchy, swollen or crusted eyelids, light sensitivity, eyelid observation, and clinician-documented eyelid-hygiene discussion.
assessment.rationale: Supports blepharitis identity, watery or red eyes, grittiness, burning or stinging, greasy or itchy eyelids, swelling, crusting, selected eyelid findings, and clinician-documented hygiene discussion.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports blepharitis identity, watery or red eyes, gritty, burning, stinging, greasy, itchy, swollen or crusted eyelids, light sensitivity, eyelid observation, and clinician-documented eyelid-hygiene discussion.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Swollen Eyelid – 40

referral.text: Supports clinician-led referral, worsening, vision change, severe redness, severe pain, photophobia, spread, trauma, uncertain diagnosis, and refractory-symptom documentation without automatically concluding urgency.
referral.rationale: Supports clinician-led return, worsening, vision-change, severe-pain, and arranged follow-up documentation.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led referral, worsening, vision change, severe redness, severe pain, photophobia, spread, trauma, uncertain diagnosis, and refractory-symptom documentation without automatically concluding urgency.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Swollen Eyelid – 40

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports contact-lens history plus clinician-assessed visual-acuity, discharge, conjunctival, corneal, and pupil fields.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source_limitations: Contact history, trauma, external-eye and extended eye examination, fluorescein, eye swab, contact-lens advice, medicine, fixed one-to-two-day review, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not recommend antibiotics, steroids, drops, or referral unless the clinician documents them.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
